title: SBA Artificial Intelligence Utilization Act of 2026
action: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Small Business and Entrepreneurship.
summary: The SBA Artificial Intelligence Utilization Act of 2026 is a law that aims to promote the use and development of artificial intelligence in the Small Business Administration (SBA) to benefit small businesses. It likely sets guidelines and regulations for the adoption and implementation of AI technologies at the SBA.

title: FARM AI Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on Agriculture.
summary: The FARM AI Act of 2026 is a policy document that likely aims to regulate or impact the use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in the agricultural sector.

title: American Artificial Intelligence Leadership and Uniformity Act
action: Referred to the Subcommittee on Oversight and Investigations.
summary: The American Artificial Intelligence Leadership and Uniformity Act aims to promote leadership and uniformity in AI development and use in the United States. This act likely outlines policies and regulations for the responsible development and implementation of AI technologies.

title: Stop AI Price Gouging and Wage Fixing Act of 2025
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committees on the Judiciary, and Education and Workforce, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Stop AI Price Gouging and Wage Fixing Act of 2025 is a proposed law aimed at preventing unfair practices by AI companies, likely focusing on regulating their pricing and compensation policies. This act seeks to promote fairness and transparency in the rapidly developing field of artificial intelligence.

title: Deterring American AI Model Theft Act of 2026
action: Ordered to be Reported (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 43 - 0.
summary: The Deterring American AI Model Theft Act of 2026 is a policy aimed at preventing the theft of AI models in the United States. It seeks to establish laws and regulations to safeguard intellectual property and protect the creators of AI technology from unauthorized use.

title: Research and Oversight of AI in Courts Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
summary: The Research and Oversight of AI in Courts Act of 2026 aims to regulate the use of artificial intelligence in court proceedings, likely focusing on ensuring accuracy, fairness, and transparency in its application. This act is intended to promote research and oversight to address the potential risks and benefits of AI in the judicial system.

title: Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act
action: Ordered to be Reported in the Nature of a Substitute (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 35 - 0.
summary: The Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act is a policy aiming to safeguard consumers from misleading or deceitful AI-related activities. It seeks to establish guidelines and regulations to prevent exploitation through AI.

title: Federal Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Federal Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Act of 2026 appears to be a legislative document designed to manage and mitigate risks associated with artificial intelligence technologies, possibly regulating their use by the government.

title: Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act
action: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 619.
summary: This policy, the Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act, aims to promote and facilitate the use of artificial intelligence in the financial services industry. The exact details and objectives of the policy are unknown due to the lack of a provided description.

title: HBCU AI Research Leadership Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The HBCU AI Research Leadership Act prioritizes the development of Artificial Intelligence at Historically Black Colleges and Universities. This legislation likely seeks to empower HBCUs in AI research by providing resources and funding for their programs.

title: K–12 AI Literacy and Readiness Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on Education and Workforce.
summary: The K-12 AI Literacy and Readiness Act of 2026 aims to introduce education on artificial intelligence to students in elementary and high schools. This legislation aims to prepare students for a future where AI is increasingly prevalent in society.

title: Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: This law aims to shield consumers from deceitful AI practices. It appears to regulate and prevent AI-related scams or misinformation.

title: READ AI Models Act
action: Ordered to be Reported in the Nature of a Substitute (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 35 - 0.
summary: The READ AI Models Act is a legislation that has been enacted to provide transparency and guidelines for artificial intelligence models, likely establishing a framework for their development, testing, and deployment in a responsible manner.
